pattern: The string is valid exactly when every 'a' precedes every 'b', which is equivalent to saying there is at most one transition point from an 'a'-run into a 'b'-run.
hints: Scan the string once and pay attention to what happens the moment a 'b' first shows up.
Keep a flag that flips on as soon as you see a 'b'; while that flag is set, seeing an 'a' afterward means the string is invalid.
A valid string looks like a block of 'a' characters (possibly empty) followed by a block of 'b' characters (possibly empty) — reject as soon as an 'a' appears after the first 'b'.

constraints.note: s consists only of the characters 'a' and 'b'

edge_inputs.description: a-run followed by a longer b-run
a single 'b' immediately precedes the only 'a'
an 'a' sneaks in after some 'b's have already appeared
long alternation with a's and b's swapping repeatedly
max length 100, clean a-run then b-run (50/50)
max length 100, single leading 'b' then all 'a' — invalid at the very first character

stress.description: random a/b string at the max constraint length
stress.generator_python:
def gen(rng, size):
    n = max(1, min(size, 100))
    s = ''.join(rng.choice('ab') for _ in range(n))
    return [s]
